cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out when Richard could have gone to the bakery. The question says that the bakery was closed after 7pm, so Richard must have gone to the bakery some time before 7pm. Let me look at the timeline of where Richard was seen. Then David saw him at the electronic store fixing a computer from 2pm to 4pm. After that, Mary saw him taking photos near the Eiffel Tower from 4pm to 5pm. Then Hannah saw him on a rooftop from 5pm to 6pm. Finally, Susan saw him driving to the water park from 6pm to 7pm. So let me list out the times he was occupied: - 8am: awake - 9am to 2pm: library - 2pm to 4pm: electronic store - 4pm to 5pm: Eiffel Tower - 5pm to 6pm: rooftop - 6pm to 7pm: driving to water park Now, the bakery was closed after 7pm, so he must have gone before that. The question is, between what times could he have gone? Looking at the timeline, the latest time he was doing something else was from 6pm to 7pm driving to the water park. But since the bakery is closed after 7pm, the latest he could have gone is before 7pm. However, the driving to the water park was from 6pm to 7pm. So during that time (6pm-7pm), he was driving. Therefore, he couldn't have gone to the bakery during that time because he was already driving to the water park. So the latest time he could have gone to the bakery is before 6pm. Now, let's check the times when he was doing other activities. From 9am to 2pm, he was at the library. So he couldn't have gone to the bakery during that time. From 2pm to 4pm, he was at the electronic store. From 4pm to 5pm, at Eiffel Tower. From 5pm to 6pm, on a rooftop. So the only time when he wasn't doing any of these activities is from 8am to 9am (when he woke up at 8am and before he went to the library at 9am), and also possibly between 5pm to 6pm, but wait, Hannah saw him on the rooftop from 5pm to 6pm, so he was occupied then. So during 5pm to 6pm, he was on the rooftop. So he couldn't go to the bakery then. So the only free time he had was from 8am to 9am. Because after that, he was occupied until 7pm. So during 8am to 9am, he could have gone to the bakery. Also, what about during the times when he was in between activities? Like between 2pm (when he finished at the electronic store) and 4pm? No, David saw him at the electronic store from 2pm to 4pm. Between 4pm and 5pm, Mary saw him at Eiffel Tower. Between 5pm and 6pm, Hannah saw him on the rooftop. Between 6pm and 7pm, Susan saw him driving to the water park. So there's no gap between these times; he was continuously occupied from 9am to 7pm, except maybe between 8am and 9am. Therefore, the only time he could have gone to the bakery is between 8am and 9am.
